name: W4 Legs Morning Flow
description: 15 min hip + squat prep. Different from W3 — no crow pose, add pigeon prep and a deep malasana hold. KEY DIFFERENCE today: your legs are not pre-cooked. This flow should feel easier and looser than W3.

exercises.notes: Standing, big slow circles. Feel the hip socket open up.
Squat to standing, hands at toes. This week go SLOWER — 3 seconds down, 2 seconds in the hole, drive up. Groove the pattern.
Low lunge, back knee down, reach inside arm toward front foot and twist. Hip flexor + thoracic.
45s each side. Front shin parallel or angled, sink into it. This is a deeper hip opener than anything in W3's flow. Breathe.
30s each side. Internal and external hip rotation. You know this one — go deeper than last week.
Deep side lunge alternating. Same as W3 but you've been doing cossack squats for 2 weeks — you have more range now. Use it.
Feet hip-width, drive hips up, squeeze glutes at top. Activates glutes before you squat — reduces knee stress.
Opposite arm and leg extend slowly. Lower back stays down. Core bracing before loading a barbell.
20s. Arms overhead, legs hovering. Same as W3. Just do it.

tips: 15 min — pigeon pose is new this week (deeper than lizard). Take your time in it.
Your legs are FRESH today (no KB yesterday). This flow should feel noticeably easier than W3 legs day.
Glute bridge before squats: activates the glutes so they share the load. Reduces the knee-dominant pattern.
Malasana: slower this week. You've drilled the squat pattern for 3 weeks — now feel it.
If short on time: hip warmup + pigeon + glute bridge. 8 min minimum.
